data.title: Mueller Industries Director John B Hansen Sells 1,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameHansen John B
RelationshipDirector, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$105,710
SEC FilingForm 4

John B Hansen, a 10% owner and director at Mueller Industries, sold 1,000 shares of common stock on October 23, 2025, at a price of $105.71 per share, totaling $105,710. Following the transaction, Hansen directly owns 91,664 shares and indirectly owns 15,000 shares through a trust benefiting his family.

SEC Filing: MUELLER INDUSTRIES INC [ MLI ] - Form 4 - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: Some UK bond market players look under-prepared for crisis, BoE policymaker warns
data.description:
Key points:
  • BoE's Hall warns of risks from non-bank financial firms
  • 'Low levels of self-insurance' could require bailout
  • BoE has warned of danger from AI bubble, private credit
  • Central bank says international cooperation needed on risks
  • BoE last intervened in bond market in October 2022

By David Milliken

Some businesses that operate in the British government bond market have made only limited preparations for a future crisis, potentially increasing the need for a bailout, Bank of England policymaker Jonathan Hall warned on Thursday.

Hall, an external member of the BoE's Financial Policy Committee and former fund manager and Goldman Sachs partner, said there was a risk of a repeat of the 2008 financial crisis where the public had to bail out banks to avoid an even bigger financial crash.

Tighter regulation of banking since then had led risk-taking to shift to less-regulated hedge funds and money market funds based outside Britain, he said.

"There are non-bank financial institutions who are increasingly important to the functioning of systemic markets like gilts but are outside of the UK regulatory perimeter and have low levels of self-insurance," Hall said in a speech to the Confederation of British Industry.

"If the public perceives that a market intervention ends up supporting these firms in stress, then the public outcry seen in the global financial crisis could be repeated but targeted against these non-banks," he added.

On Tuesday, BoE Governor Andrew Bailey said the recent collapses of U.S. auto parts maker First Brands had echoes of the start of the global financial crisis. The BoE has also warned that AI speculation was pushing up share prices in a way similar to the dotcom bubble at the start of the millennium.

"Pricing is quite optimistic," Hall said.

BOE INTERVENED IN BOND MARKET IN 2022

The BoE bought 19.3 billion pounds ($25.9 billion) of British government debt in 2022 after bond prices slumped after Prime Minister Liz Truss' budget proposals. Hall said it was justifiable at the time as it was not reasonable to have expected funds to prepare for such a big move.

Since then, Hall said the BoE had taken measures to design market support mechanisms that only helped non-bank firms which were well regulated - such as pension funds, liability-driven investment funds and insurers.

But that would not always be sufficient in a crisis, and the next approach was to ensure that any BoE intervention was priced and timed in a way that excessively risky firms failed before the BoE intervened, he added.

However, continued international cooperation was also needed to ensure foreign businesses active in British markets were appropriately regulated, he said.

"It would be worrying if this important work to build resilience were threatened by reduced global cooperation, as this would unfairly shift the cost of insurance from overseas firms to UK institutions and the UK public," he said.

($1 = 0.7451 pounds)

data.title: ICE Midday: Crude Oil Rally Lifts Canola
data.description:
WINNIPEG — Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange received a boost on Thursday. The United States and the European Union imposed new sanctions on Russian oil companies in attempts to bring Russia back to peace talks.

As a result, crude oil surged nearly US$3 per barrel, supporting vegetable oils. Chicago soyoil and Malaysian palm oil were up, while European rapeseed was mostly higher.

The Canadian dollar was up less than one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared to Wednesday's close.

About 26,300 canola contracts have traded at 10:06 CDT. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne:

Price Change

Nov 620.80 up 7.70

Jan 634.80 up 7.30

Mar 646.60 up 7.10

May 657.50 up 7.20

Source: MarketsFarm

data.title: Redeye: Ependion - Blended Q3, minority stake in cybersecurity company
data.description:
Redeye was hoping for a bit more in terms of topline, but market demand has been hesitant, particularly within the train segment. On the bright side margins still improved, HMI products are showing good growth and newly acquired Welotec is performing well. Furthermore, Ependion is taking a minority stake in RazorSecure, a software provider of cybersecurity solutions for the train industry. Overall, the near-term outlook remains uncertain and we are now somewhat more cautious in our assumptions for market recovery.

data.title: UnitedHealth's Optum unit CFO has stepped down, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
The finance chief of UnitedHealth Group's Optum unit, Roger Connor, has stepped down after less than six months in the role, Bloomberg news reported on Thursday, citing an internal memo.

This marks another change in the healthcare conglomerate's top management under UnitedHealth CEO Stephen Hemsley as he helms a turnaround at the company after several setbacks.

UnitedHealth did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment. Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

Connor, who was named chief financial officer of the Optum health services unit in May, is leaving the role to return to the UK, Bloomberg News reported.

Ben Eklo, a longtime finance executive at the company, will take over as Optum's finance chief on November 1, the report added.

The Optum unit includes the company's pharmacy benefits business, a vast portfolio of medical clinics and in-home care programs, and a data and technology unit.

data.title: US appeals court blocks FTC subpoena targeting Media Matters for now
data.description:
By Mike Scarcella

A U.S. appeals court on Thursday declined for now to reinstate a U.S. Federal Trade Commission subpoena that sought information from nonprofit Media Matters as the agency probes whether media watchdog groups coordinated advertising boycotts against some social media platforms.

A divided three-judge panel of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit upheld an August decision by a lower court judge who had called the subpoena retaliatory and described it as a "fishing expedition" by the FTC.

The appeals court ruling was a setback for the Republican-led FTC, which under Chairman Andrew Ferguson had sought internal information from Media Matters about its communications with other groups about labeling published content as misinformation or hate speech.

Circuit Judges Patricia Millett and Robert Wilkins found the FTC had not shown it was likely to win on the merits. In a dissent, Circuit Judge Justin Walker said Media Matters had only "sparse" evidence that the subpoena was improper.

The FTC and Media Matters did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

Media Matters, a Washington, D.C.-based liberal advocacy group, came under scrutiny by Elon Musk and some Republicans over its reporting on advertising on X after the billionaire entrepreneur bought the social media platform.

Musk's X sued Media Matters in 2023, accusing the organization of defaming it in an article that said ads for major brands had appeared next to posts on X that touted far-right extremist content.

Media Matters has denied any wrongdoing. It accused X of "abusive" tactics in a countersuit, and separately sued the FTC after it launched its investigation into the group.

In its lawsuit against the FTC, Media Matters in June urged U.S. District Judge Sparkle Sooknanan to “put an end to the latest effort by the Trump Administration and Elon Musk’s government allies to punish, intimidate, and harass Media Matters for publishing reporting they do not like.”

Sooknanan in her order blocking the FTC’s subpoena said Media Matters was likely to prevail in the lawsuit.

The FTC warned in its appeal that Sooknanan’s order was "unprecedented" and said that if it was left in place, “civil and criminal investigations undertaken by dozens of federal agencies could be called into question and subject to judicial delay and second-guessing.”

Media Matters told the appeals court that the FTC subpoena "undermined the organization’s operations and caused third parties to shun it."

Read more:

Musk’s X can sue watchdog Media Matters in Texas, US judge rules

US judge tosses Rumble lawsuit claiming advertising boycott

FTC probes Media Matters over Musk's X boycott claims, document shows

data.title: Trump Pardons Convicted Binance Founder — WSJ
data.description:
By Rebecca Ballhaus, Josh Dawsey, Patricia Kowsmann and Angus Berwick

President Trump has pardoned Changpeng Zhao, the convicted founder of the crypto exchange Binance, according to people familiar with the matter, following months of efforts by Zhao to boost the Trump family's own crypto company.

The president signed the pardon on Wednesday, the people said. Trump recently indicated to advisers that he was sympathetic to arguments of political persecution related to Zhao and others, one of the people said.

White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said that Trump had "exercised his constitutional authority by issuing a pardon for Mr. Zhao, who was prosecuted by the Biden Administration in their war on cryptocurrency." She added: "The Biden Administration's war on crypto is over."

Binance didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

A pardon will likely pave the way for Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange, to return to the U.S., after the company pleaded guilty in 2023 to violating U.S. anti-money-laundering requirements and was barred from operating there.

The company has spent nearly a year pursuing a pardon for Zhao, who left prison in September 2024 after serving a four-month sentence for related charges. Earlier this year, the company hired lobbyist Ches McDowell to help pursue a pardon, the Journal previously reported.

Since Trump's election, Binance has also been a key supporter of his family's World Liberty Financial crypto venture, a business that has driven a huge leap in the president's personal wealth.

data.title: Oil Rally Opens Window for Producers to Hedge — Market Talk
data.description: The oil price rally as the U.S. tightens sanctions on Russia provides a window for producers to hedge their production before the looming supply glut and the administration's push for lower oil prices ahead of 2026 mid-term elections, Citi's Francesco Martoccia says in a note. While the sharpest moves are at the front of the curve, WTI futures for 2026 have moved back above $60 a barrel, he writes. But the hedging window may close quickly. "We believe the geopolitical premium is set to erode, because the enforcement gaps could widen and the supply chain of shadow fleets and middlemen adapts, and especially as the loosening oil market fundamentals become more evident in the data." WTI is up 4.8% at $61.30 a barrel and Brent is 4.6% higher at $65.46 a barrel.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Canada to Acquire 15% Stake in Toronto-Area Small-Modular Nuclear Project
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada said it would acquire a 15% stake in a nuclear-power project near Toronto that's fueled by small-modular reactors.

Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday said the government would inject 2 billion Canadian dollars, or the equivalent of $1.43 billion, into the Darlington New Nuclear Project, owned and operated by provincially owned Ontario Power Generation. The project, billed as North America's first commercial power plant fueled by small-modular reactors, received regulatory approval in May.

Canada has encouraged the development of small-modular reactors this decade. These reactors are smaller versions of legacy nuclear reactors, and can be transported and dropped into locales where they are either hooked up to the electricity grid to serve a large region or meet the needs of industrial areas or small towns.

Carney said the C$2 billion investment is a sign of the type of measures his government will unveil next month in its annual budget plan to help ramp up the construction of infrastructure and resource projects, and bolster business investment from depressed levels.

Last month, Carney listed the nuclear project as one of five so-called nation-building projects the government would throw its weight behind to ensure their completion.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Tractor Supply after quarterly results beat estimates on seasonal demand
data.description:
** Shares of farming equipment retailer Tractor Supply <TSCO.O> rise about 5% at $57.69

** TSCO beats third quarter profit and comparable sales estimates helped by rise in demand for seasonal products

** Reports qtrly profit of 49 cents per share vs estimates of 48 cents per share - data compiled by LSEG

** Comparable sales rise in the range 4.6% and 5.6% beating estimates of a 5.3% rise

** "The traffic improvement likely indicates that Tractor has finally digested the massive sales growth from the pandemic," says brokerage Truist Securities

** However, Co forecasts its full-year sales and profit largely below estimates, hurt by cautious spending among rural consumers for big-ticket items

** As of last close, TSCO up nearly 3.4% YTD

data.title: Street View: Kinder Morgan sees mixed sentiments on Q3 results
data.description:
** U.S. pipeline operator Kinder Morgan posted a rise in third-quarter profit on Wednesday, helped by higher volumes of natural gas transported through its pipelines

** Shares of the company were down 3.2% at $26.68

BROKERAGES SEE BACKLOG HEADWINDS OFFSET BY INCREASED DEMAND

** Jefferies ("Hold", PT: $30) says despite a strong Q3, investor attention should persist on unsanctioned backlog materializing

** TD Cowen ("Buy", PT: $34) says KMI's backlog includes mostly smaller projects, though a few large ones, which could mean a majority of the value associated with it is contingent on a few project wins

** RBC Capital Markets ("Sector Perform", PT: $28) says it believes KMI will benefit over the long term from increasing natural gas demand, driven by LNG export capacity growth and power-related demand

** UBS ("Buy", PT: $38) also says it sees KMI in a positive light owing to growing demand for natgas for both power needs and expansion of LNG facilities on the Gulf Coast

data.title: Catena adjusts their growth and sustainability targets
data.description:
Catena

Catena AB's Board of Directors has decided to adjust certain company targets in its business plan for 2026.

23 October 2025 5.20 p.m. CEST

The previously communicated objective of achieving at least 10 percent total growth annually is to be replaced by the following targets:

  • Profit from property management shall achieve at least 10 percent in annual compounded growth rate per share over a five-year period
  • The company shall generate an average annual compounded growth rate of at least 12 percent per share in net asset value (NRV) over a five-year period

The Board has also decided to adjust the sustainability target of net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2030. The new target is net zero GHG emissions, across all scopes, by 2040, with an interim target of at least 50 percent by 2030. Our objectives regarding biodiversity remain unchanged, while the certification target and the objective of Catena exerting a positive influence on stakeholders and the environment by participating in societal development will, as before, not be included in the business plan. These objectives remain but are now deemed more to be interim goals and activities on the way to achieving our ultimate targets.

“We welcome clearly defined targets that focus on the values we create for owners, customers and society, and support Catena’s overarching objective to generate a strong cash flow from operating activities to enable sustainable growth and stable returns,” says Catena CEO Jörgen Eriksson.

The dividend policy and finance policy remain unchanged.

For further information, please contact:

Jörgen Eriksson, CEO, Tel. +46 (0)730 70 22 42,  jorgen.eriksson@catena.se

Amanda Thynell,  Head of sustainability, Tel. +46 (0)725 10 03 01 amanda.thynell@catena.se

Follow us: catena.se / LinkedIn

This is information that Catena AB (publ) is obliged to publish under the EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) 596/2014. The information was provided by the above contacts for publication at the aforementioned time.

About Catena

Catena is a listed property company that sustainably develops and durably manages efficient logistics facilities through collaboration. Its strategically located properties supply the Scandinavian metropolitan areas and are adapted for both current and future flows of goods. The overarching objective is to generate a strong cash flow from operating activities to enable sustainable growth and stable returns. As of 30 June 2025, the properties had a total value of SEK 42,346 million. Catena shares are traded on NASDAQ Stockholm, Large Cap.

data.title: Honeywell lifts 2025 profit forecast; shares jump
data.description:
** Shares of Honeywell rise nearly 7% to about $220.9 in early trading, after co lifts 2025 profit forecast

** Says increased orders across its business segments pushed the company's total backlog to another record high

** Co now expects 2025 adj. EPS of between $10.60 and $10.70 compared to $10.24 and $10.44 earlier

** Q3 adj. EPS came in at $2.82, beating estimates of $2.57 - data compiled by LSEG

** Quarterly revenue of $10.41 bln vs. expectation of $10.14 bln

** Including session moves, shares down about 2.2% YTD

data.title: Candy maker Tootsie Roll posts higher Q3 sales, warns of margin pressure from input costs
data.description:
Overview

  • Tootsie Roll Q3 2025 net sales rise 3% yr/yr, driven by successful marketing programs

  • Q3 2025 net earnings increase 9% yr/yr, aided by higher investment income

  • Higher input costs, especially cocoa, impact Tootsie Roll's profit margins in 2025

Outlook

  • Company expects high cocoa and chocolate costs to continue into Q4 2025 and 2026

  • Company remains cautious about tariff impacts on costs

  • Tootsie Roll investing in manufacturing to meet new product demands

Result Drivers

  • MARKETING SUCCESS - Successful marketing and sales programs, including pre-Halloween efforts, contributed to higher sales in Q3 2025

  • HIGHER INPUT COSTS - Increased costs for cocoa and chocolate affected profit margins, with expectations of continued pressure into Q4 2025 and 2026

  • INVESTMENT INCOME - Net earnings benefited from increased investment income and a favorable bond accretion adjustment

Key Details

Metric

Q3 EPS

Beat/Miss

Actual

$0.49

Consensus Estimate

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact RefinitivNewsSupport@thomsonreuters.com.

data.title: Ribbon Communications Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Ribbon Communications Inc., a global provider of communications technology and network solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, showcasing a mix of stability and growth in key areas.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $215.4 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, driven by growth in the IP Optical Networks segment.
  • Gross Profit: $108.0 million, maintaining stability compared to the prior year, indicating effective cost management.
  • Income (loss) from operations: $2.8 million, showing a positive turnaround from a loss in the previous year, highlighting improved operational efficiency.
  • Net loss: $(12.1) million, a reduction in loss compared to the previous year, suggesting progress in financial performance.
  • Loss per share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.07), consistent with the previous year, indicating stable shareholder impact despite net losses.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Cloud and Edge segment generated $124.4 million in revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $369.1 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The IP Optical Networks segment reported $90.9 million in revenue for the three months and $248.2 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remains a significant market, contributing $96.7 million in revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025. Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) generated $62.9 million, while Asia Pacific contributed $43.3 million during the same period.
  • Customer Segments: Sales to enterprise customers accounted for $37.5 million in product revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025, while sales to service provider customers contributed $72.5 million.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company continues to focus on streamlining operations through strategic restructuring programs, including workforce reductions and facilities consolidation, to align with current sales levels and support investment in growth areas.
  • Future Outlook: The company is committed to enhancing its cloud-centric solutions and expanding its global presence, with a focus on providing secure and high-performance communications technology to service providers and enterprises.

SEC Filing: Ribbon Communications Inc. [ RBBN ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Index Hits 3-1/2-Year High
data.description:
The Kansas City Fed’s Manufacturing Production Index climbed to 15 in October 2025, the highest since April 2022, from 4 in September, indicating a notable pickup in manufacturing expansion.

Manufacturing activity saw slight gains in both durable and nondurable sectors.

Printing and paper boosted nondurable growth, and metal manufacturing supported durable gains.

Month-over-month indexes were mostly positive, aside from new export orders and the average workweek, with production and shipments rising moderately.

Year-over-year, most indexes were negative, except supplier delivery times and capital expenditures, and production and employment indexes remained in negative territory.

Expectations for future activity improved, as the composite index increased from 7 to 14, though anticipated new orders and employment for the next six months remained stable.
